review_lens: Ignore-asymmetry (CONFIRMED): F14 (IJOAO8), F17 (IJOERU), F39 (IJONYD) — DefUse::VisitArraySubscriptExpr missing. F46 (IJOSI3) HIGH — `getMemberFullField` strips ImplicitCastExpr but not ParenExpr; `(s).b` paren-wrap bypasses move tracking, runtime double-free

SafeExpr-strip gap (CONFIRMED): F62 (HIGH, move-through-borrow), F65 (null-narrowing). `IgnoreParenCasts()`/`IgnoreParenImpCasts()` peel the standard wrapper set from `IgnoreExpr.h` but NOT BSC's `SafeExpr` (`_Safe(...)`/`_Unsafe(...)`). Any check that strips wrappers then dispatches on the unwrapped node mis-handles a SafeExpr-wrapped operand.

Codegen emitter missing VisitSafeExpr (CONFIRMED): F60 (aggregate), F63 (complex). A `CGExpr*Emitter` (AggExprEmitter, ComplexExprEmitter) lacks a `VisitSafeExpr` override, so `_Safe(<typed-expr>)` falls to the unsupported-expression fallback. Scalar emitters got the recurse-into-subexpr override; aggregate + complex did not.

Codegen drops side-effecting builtin arg (CANDIDATE): F58 — `BI__assume_initialized` handler returns a no-op without emitting its argument; side effects in the arg vanish. One incident; promote to CONFIRMED on a second.

description: isNullExpr lacks ConditionalOperator arm: both-arms-null ternary init of _Owned ptr -> spurious ownership 'memory leak' (false positive)
grep_rule: rg -c ConditionalOperator clang/lib/AST/BSC/ExprBSC.cpp  # defect: 0 (isNullExpr has no ConditionalOperator arm); siblings clang/lib/Analysis/BSC/BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:344 and clang/lib/Sema/SemaDecl.cpp:14776 each have a ConditionalOperatorClass case

description: BSC AddressSpaceShift=12 (20-bit storage) < MaxAddressSpace=0x1fffff (21-bit): high target address space silently truncated, accepted then mis-rendered as address_space(0)
grep_rule: python3 -c "sh=12; mx=0x1fffff; st=32-sh; print('DEFECT' if st < mx.bit_length() else 'ok')"  # BSC Type.h: AddressSpaceShift=12 -> 20-bit storage, MaxAddressSpace=0x1fffff -> 21-bit; storage<needed => high AS truncates. Check: rg -n 'AddressSpaceShift = 12|MaxAddressSpace = 0x1fffff' clang/include/clang/AST/Type.h

description: ensure_init_if_ret delegation credit dropped for _Owned (Move) params — spurious 'cannot verify contract' on safe delegation
grep_rule: rg -n 'asCopiedLocal' clang/lib/Analysis/BSC/BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp  # defect: the delegation-recognition predicate (called at lines 529, 563) returns None for Operand::Move; the builder marks _Owned args Move at BSCIRBuilder.cpp:1020. Check: rg -n 'Op.K == Operand::Copy' clang/lib/Analysis/BSC/BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp  (asCopiedLocal @77) vs rg -n 'Arg.K = Operand::Move' clang/lib/Analysis/BSC/BSCIRBuilder.cpp

description: ensure_init/ensure_init_if_ret deref-read check matches only the Deref projection, silently accepting out[0]/out[i] reads of the un-initialized pointee
grep_rule: rg -n 'Projections\[0\].K != ProjectionElem::Deref' clang/lib/Analysis/BSC/BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp  # defect: line 1463 is the sole != Deref early-return in checkEnsureInitDerefReads; sibling deref forms Index/ConstantIndex (emitted by BSCIRBuilder.cpp:142-155 for out[0]/out[i]) bypass it. Compare: rg -n 'createConstantIndex|createIndex' clang/lib/Analysis/BSC/BSCIRBuilder.cpp

review_lens: Visit/switch coverage gap (CONFIRMED): F09 (IJO88R), F18 (IJOEWJ). F47 (IJOSRL) HIGH — `CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak` recognized-temp set excludes CompoundLiteralExpr; member access on compound-literal-with-owned-field silently leaks

description: ensure_init/ensure_init_if_ret pointee read in a terminator operand (call arg / switch-if-while-for discriminant) silently accepted
grep_rule: rg -n 'checkEnsureInitDerefReads' clang/lib/Analysis/BSC/BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp  # defect: only ONE call site (line 1833, inside the Statement::Assign loop); the terminator-operand block at 1840-1853 calls only checkOperand on CD.Args/CD.Callee/T.getSwitchInt().Discriminant and never checkEnsureInitDerefReads. Compare: rg -n 'checkOperand\(CD.Args|checkOperand\(T.getSwitchInt' clang/lib/Analysis/BSC/BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp
id: C3 (Visit/switch coverage gap) — checkEnsureInitDerefReads invoked only from the Assign-statement loop, never for terminator operands

review_lens: Prologue/Epilogue asymmetry (CONFIRMED): F09 (IJO88R) codegen + F16 (IJOEJP) analysis + F40 (IJOPPE) short-circuit/ternary hoisting

Dataflow merge state hole (CONFIRMED): F26 (IJOK0G) — mergeDPVD asymmetric meet; mergeVD/mergeFP same shape but protected by initStatus pre-population. F44 (IJOSGF) — `checkSUse`/`isAddrMut` collapses per-field Null state into Owned (single-statement collapse, not cross-branch merge). F45 (IJOSHF) HIGH — `checkSFieldAssign` doesn't erase from SNullOwnedFields when reassigning a previously-null field; stale entry combined with F44 migration produces runtime double-free

description: ensure_init_if_ret delegation through a conditional-expression return is wrongly rejected (PendingCondInits intersection merge drops the PCI at the ternary branch join)
grep_rule: rg -n 'Merge PendingCondInits: intersection' clang/lib/Analysis/BSC/BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp  # defect: line 766-777 intersects PendingCondInits (is_contained at :770), dropping a delegation PCI created on only one arm of a conditional-expression return (VisitConditionalOperator, BSCIRBuilder.cpp:1258). Compare the union-merge sibling at :740 (ReassignedParams). Distinct from C15 grep 'asCopiedLocal' @529/563 (call-arg Move-drop, needs _Owned) — this fires with plain int*.
id: C5 (dataflow merge state hole) — PendingCondInits intersection merge at BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:766 drops a delegation PCI created on only one arm of a conditional-expression return; distinct from C15 (call-arg Move-drop, needs _Owned) and the 4 documented §3.7.5 limitations

review_lens: Localized check skipped on wrappers (CONFIRMED): F13 (LOW, not filed); F12 (IJO88U) — narrow flow variant
Narrowing not propagated across join (CONFIRMED): F12 (IJO88U)
Path-identity aliasing gap (CONFIRMED): F42 (IJOQGO) — borrow checker's string-based path comparison doesn't model union aliasing

Type-recursion gap (FunctionProto / nested) (CONFIRMED): F41 (IJOPV7) owned-fnptr outer-level only; F53 redecl `HasDiffBorrorOrOwnedQualifiers` walks PointerType pointee but not FunctionProtoType params; F56 `HasDiffNullabilityQualifiers`; F57 `AreOwnedBorrowQualifiersCompatible`. A type-walking predicate recurses through pointer pointees but stops at function-proto param/return types — qualifiers nested in a fnptr param are invisible.
